## [00:00 - 03:01]

This is Deepash and this video is all about the automation accelerator. What is that all about? So this module, this training is all about how you use rules and optimization. So what I want you to do is to take this video into account that this is not absolutely instructional because the rules and optimizations will differ per account. Test your optimization manually and get your rules right before you start automating. And so for example, take a look at the add sniper strategy. What I take you through the video is how to manually optimize your ad sets. I would absolutely 100% recommend you go through that and manually understand how your account is performing before trying to automate any of that as well. And whether you're kind of pausing or increasing ad spend, just make sure you know the rules manually and what works for your account before you automate it. You could also test using Facebook's internal rules and you to test basic strategies. So you can be alerted when something happens to see if it was the right decision to set the threshold. For example, I scale using a tool called reveal bot. There are many rules engines out there. There is a link below. If you want to have a look at reveal bots, and I'll take you through kind of typical rules that I put in place for ad sniper, VNH scaling and the graduation framework. However, before we start, absolutely avoid overconfidence is one of the most dangerous forms of revenue loss. And I say that because I've been there and I've done that. I don't want you to do the same. What that means is if you're confident, you know, you've been doing things manually, and you now want to go into automation, you want to set some rules, etc. And you want to sit back and enjoy Facebook growth with, you know, 50%, 75% less effort. It can happen. You can set rules that make a huge, huge operational difference to your profitability and scaling, but one wrong move and you will lose a lot of money. So when you're setting up these rules, make sure you're testing them, testing them, monitoring them in your absolutely confident day after day, week after week that they're working before you decide that you're going to take a slight step back and let the machine do its thing. And the reason is because tools like reveal bot or Facebook zones or automation tools are only as good as your input. So if you make one mistake because you thought you're doing one thing, and the tool does something else, which was unexpected, you could quite easily make some big losses.

50%. So for example, if my CPA target $25, I will give it up to $37.5 before I pause it. Now the reason is because during the 1 to 5pm, you might get some variation. ## [18:16 - 21:17]

the target of my CPA and I've had more than one sound or the spend is five times the target CPA, then I'll pull it out now that five times will depend on your risk tolerance as well. So, you know, if you're if you're doing high spend, you might want to limit that to three acts. If you're doing low spend, you might want to just give it a bit more breathing space. But just make sure you do set up the stop loss to make sure that you don't lose money on assets which are spinning out spinning out of control as well. Then the final one I'll take you through for optimization is the anti void, which I call the anti void. This is where you're you're running ad sniper strategy, you're pausing ads, and then you end up with an ad set which has zero ads running. So you could have an audience which generally performs well, but for some reason, you might have had a bad day and all the ads have been paused because of bad performance, you want those to be pulled back online as well. So what I prefer to do is to be alerted if there are no ads currently running for an ad set at any time of the day, because then I want to go back in and either restart the same ads and give them another chance on my restart or just duplicate because if you're just plain restart, it might get paused again, or I might just load up some new ads from my graduation. So this one I would do an alert if there's zero running ads in the ad set today, and the impressions today is zero. So for example, there were some ads running yesterday, they're no longer running, or the impressions yesterday is greater than 500. So I want to make sure that I'm capturing ads and I'm bringing them back in if if an ad set is now completely paused. Then I'm going to go into scale. So within this, I'm looking at the V and H scaling strategies and how I can develop rules for those. The first part is V scaling. So this is a kind of moderate V scale. This is increasing the budget 20% at 9am market time only, if the yesterday's CPA below target. And the last three days CPA below target. So we've had good stable performance, and then kind of making sure we've had enough acquisitions as well. Now, why do I run it at nine o'clock and not two o'clock in the morning or three o'clock or anything like that? Generally, it's because when I'm running multiple accounts, I want to be online when the budget increases are going on, just to be able to track it as well. So that's the only reason I choose 9am. If you're confident you should be doing it at one to a year, because that's the best time to be increasing budgets. The reason not to do it at 12am is because the algorithm may not have counted as you know, some of the transactions that may have come through just after 12am and may be counted to the previous day. So run it to 3am if you have to do it early on. And then if you want to be online and tracking things like I would prefer than right now, I am. So I'm just

## [21:17 - 24:18]

making sure that there's good stability before I'm increasing my budgets. Just re-instructing again that you make sure that there were actually some acquisitions, because some tools, zero acquisitions equals zero CPA. And then for Ecom, again, you can add an additional A rule for RIS. I won't have a separate slide on this because hopefully you've seen how that works before. So that's the moderate V scaling. Then there's an advanced V scaling, which I run between 2 and 5pm market time. And I increase the budget by 20% if today's CPA is below target and the last three days has been good as well. So previously I'd run this 9am rule based on yesterday. This one I'm running the 2-5pm rule based on today. So this is a really aggressive style of V scaling, which I would only recommend if you've made it work manually and you're confident in your audience size and your scalability and you want that much more aggressiveness. I would do this. I'd run 20% early on in the morning and 20% kind of mid to late afternoon and no later than 5pm because by that point you're running out of time for the pacing algorithm to be able to use your old budget effectively. So make sure that you're absolutely confident before you start to use the more kind of advanced style rules for V scaling. Then there's the V scale punisher. This is about reducing your budget 10%. And if you've been through the V scaling videos you'll understand that I use the 10% decrease as a punishment for the algorithm. So that tells the algorithm that the performance has been good, you've been spending some money and you're not happy with it now. And that goes back to the algorithm and it tries to figure out how it can maybe just try and adjust its targeting to give you better quality customers. And then the 10% number is arbitrary. It doesn't have to be 10%. Some accounts have done it at 1% as well. Others at 5%. The reason I chose 10% is if I'm increasing 20% I don't want to decrease 20% because I don't want to lose all that momentum. So the 20% increase is like two steps forward. The 10% is one step back and it just helped me to kind of balance a good budget between that as well. So again the rules are if the yesterday CPA was above the expected target plus 25% and that 25% will depend on your threshold for what you see as acceptable. And we've had more than one acquisition or we've had zero sales and spendings over CPA times five as per the previous rules as well. And again for e-com you can add an or rule for Roless as well. So the punisher strategy is something you can do automatically, something you can do manually, but again make sure that it's working for your account manually and you have confidence in that number before you start to use it as well.
